summary: Applied the ADR 0197 Dify visual workflow canvas from the isolated workstream, verified the internal Dify bootstrap and smoke workflow export on docker-runtime-lv3, repaired the governed Dify tool bridge through api.example.com, and recorded the remaining public-edge blocker caused by the external Hetzner DNS write brownout.

verification.check: Focused repository validation and syntax coverage
verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with requests --with httpx --with cryptography --with fastapi pytest tests/test_api_gateway.py tests/test_api_gateway_runtime_role.py tests/test_dify_api.py tests/test_sync_tools_to_dify.py -q` returned `25 passed`, and both `make syntax-check-dify` and `make syntax-check-api-gateway` completed successfully from the isolated worktree.

verification.check: Dify converge and internal health verification
verification.observed: The repo replay `make converge-dify EXTRA_ARGS='-e dify_skip_edge=true'` completed successfully, the local SSH tunnel to `127.0.0.1:18094` reached the live Dify runtime on `docker-runtime-lv3`, `curl http://127.0.0.1:18094/healthz` returned healthy JSON, and `curl http://127.0.0.1:18094/console/api/setup` reported `{"step":"finished","setup_at":"2026-03-28T00:11:21"}`.

verification.check: Bootstrap smoke workflow export and governed tool sync
verification.observed: `uv run --with requests --with pyyaml python3 scripts/dify_smoke.py --base-url http://127.0.0.1:18094 --admin-email operator@example.com --admin-password-file /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/dify/admin-password.txt --init-password-file /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/dify/init-password.txt --tools-api-key-file /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/dify/tools-api-key.txt` completed with `setup_step: finished`, `login_result: success`, `tool_count: 11`, and exported `platform/dify-workflows/lv3-dify-smoke.yml`.

`curl -sS -X POST https://api.example.com/v1/dify-tools/get-platform-status -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-LV3-Dify-Api-Key: $(cat /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/dify/tools-api-key.txt)" -d '{}'` returned `HTTP/2 200` with the governed platform-status payload after the gateway runtime was extended to package the workflow-contract repo surfaces and emit the correct `service_identity` audit class.

`curl https://agents.example.com/` still fails with `Could not resolve host: agents.example.com`; the repo-side runtime and governed tool bridge are live, but the public Dify edge hostname remains blocked by the external Hetzner DNS write brownout and must be replayed from `main` once that provider-side outage is resolved.

notes: The live apply was performed from the isolated worktree `/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.worktrees/ws-0197-live-apply` on branch `codex/ws-0197-live-apply` after rebasing to the latest `origin/main`.

A one-time manual OpenBao policy upsert for `lv3-service-dify-runtime` was required before the repo replay could fully converge; that manual step is intentionally recorded here so the later `main` replay is not treated as hidden context.

The Dify runtime was moved from the intended internal port `8093` to `8094` because `8093` was already occupied by the unrelated Plane proxy on `docker-runtime-lv3`; the live apply preserved Plane and updated the Dify runtime to avoid that conflict.

Protected integration files remain intentionally unchanged on this workstream branch: `VERSION`, release sections in `changelog.md`, canonical observed state in `versions/stack.yaml`, and the top-level README integrated status summary.
